Waypoints.name: 200A - Human-inspired Robotics (HiRo) Lab
Waypoints.desc: The lab creates intuitive shared-autonomy interfaces for mobile humanoid robots, advancing manipulation, adaptive interaction, AR communication, and teleoperator training.
Waypoints.modelDesc: IONA is a shared‑autonomy mobile humanoid robot designed to support remote nursing tasks. It features a height‑adjustable motorized chest, a compact 1700 mm frame with variable width, and AR/VR interfaces for flexible teleoperation.

Waypoints.name: 200B - Efficient Learning and Planning for Intelligent Systems (ELPIS) Lab
Waypoints.desc: The Lab builds autonomous robots that learn from the real world, advancing efficiency, robustness, and visual-based planning through the fusion of classical planning and modern machine learning.
Waypoints.modelDesc: This robot is a compact, torque‑controlled manipulator known for its precision and smooth motion. In this work, it was used as the primary platform for evaluating motion‑planning performance in semi‑static environments.

Waypoints.name: 200C - Novel Engineering for Swarm Technologies (NEST) Lab
Waypoints.desc: The lab researches swarm robotics with a focus on coordination algorithms, software engineering, and human-swarm interaction.
Waypoints.modelDesc: Swarm robotics explores how large groups of simple robots can coordinate through distributed control to maintain global connectivity while collectively covering and completing multiple tasks.

Waypoints.name: 200D - Manipulation and Environmental Robotics (MER) Lab
Waypoints.desc: The MER Lab advances robotic manipulation through vision, control, and machine learning, tackling environmental tasks like recycling and leading benchmarking in dexterous manipulation and active vision.
Waypoints.modelDesc: The robot is a compliant, under‑actuated manipulator designed to execute human‑inspired dexterous picking skills, and in this work it was used to grasp difficult, cluttered, and obstructed objects using learned strategies that decide when and how each skill should be applied.

Waypoints.name: 200F - Safe Autonomy and Intelligence (SAIL) Lab
Waypoints.desc: The SAIL Lab researches safety-critical control and trustworthy ML to create autonomous systems that are both intelligent and certifiable.Lab Number: 200F
Waypoints.modelDesc: This lab explores the domain of intelligent transportation systems, to include autonomous vehicles, vessels, and flights.

Waypoints.name: 200E - Adaptive and Intelligent Robotics (AIR) Lab
Waypoints.desc: The AIR Lab studies adaptive robotic systems that use real-time perception, planning, and learning for assembly, manipulation, and navigation in human-centered and industrial environments.
Waypoints.modelDesc: The robot is an adaptive manipulator designed to operate reliably in uncertain, changing environments, using real‑time perception, planning, and learning to perform tasks such as assembly, manipulation, and navigation in human‑centered settings.
